---
title: "Research"
description: "A top-level map from stable math and paper-reading skills into theorem families, active directions, surveys, and venue cultures."
page-type: module-overview
status: published
update-status: current
level: research
topic: research-map
prerequisites:
  - /paper-lab/index.qmd
  - /roadmaps/ai-ml-theory.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Understand the recommended first-pass order through the Research section."
  - "See how theorem families, directions, surveys, and venues play different roles."
  - "Connect stable topic mastery to frontier reading without skipping the paper-reading workflow."
  - "Choose the right research page for your current bottleneck."
applications:
  - research-reading
  - theory-paper-reading
  - literature-entry
  - publication-awareness
keywords:
  - research
  - theorem families
  - surveys
  - venues
  - directions
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-25
source-check-date: 2026-04-25
review-cadence: quarterly
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
stable-sources:
  - "Paper Lab"
  - "AI / ML Theory roadmap"
current-sources:
  - "Theorem Families"
  - "Directions"
  - "Surveys"
  - "Venues"
---

## Why This Section Matters

`Research` is where the site stops acting like a curriculum and starts acting like a literature map.

The job of this section is not to throw you into random current papers. Its job is to help you answer:

- what theorem family is this paper using?
- which direction does this result belong to?
- should I read a survey first?
- what kind of venue culture am I looking at?

That makes this section the layer between:

- stable math
- paper-reading workflow
- active research literature

## Recommended First-Pass Order

Use this order:

1. [Theorem Families](theorem-families.qmd)
2. [Directions](directions.qmd)
3. [Surveys](surveys.qmd)
4. [Venues](venues.qmd)

That order is deliberate:

- start with recurring theorem and proof patterns
- then see which active directions are built on top of those patterns
- then choose good literature entry points
- finally learn how venue families shape audience and evidence expectations

## When To Open Which Page

- `Theorem Families`: open when a theorem feels dense, but the bigger pattern is still unclear
- `Directions`: open when you want to know where a stable topic leads in current research
- `Surveys`: open when you need a structured entry point instead of jumping into recent papers
- `Venues`: open when you want to understand audience, evidence culture, and where a line of work tends to live

## How This Section Connects To The Rest Of The Site

- [Paper Lab](../paper-lab/index.qmd) teaches you how to read papers
- `Research` helps you choose what literature patch to read next
- [Applications](../applications/index.qmd) shows where the math reappears in modern models
- [Roadmaps](../roadmaps/index.qmd) gives you longer study paths when you need a sequencing layer

If `Paper Lab` teaches workflow, `Research` teaches orientation.

## Sources And Further Reading

- [Paper Lab](../paper-lab/index.qmd) - `First pass` - the workflow layer you should use before diving into theorem families and literature maps.
- [AI / ML Theory Roadmap](../roadmaps/ai-ml-theory.qmd) - `Second pass` - a strong route when your research interest is specifically theory-facing ML.
- [Theorem Families](theorem-families.qmd) - `Paper bridge` - the best first research page when theorem statements still feel isolated and opaque.
- [Directions](directions.qmd) - `Paper bridge` - the best next page when you want to know which frontier areas grow naturally out of the site's stable math.
